summary.intent: Fix a CI coverage failure on PR #727 (branch codex/issue-320-431-checkbox-adapters) in the fogodev/ars-ui repo by addressing a CRAP (Change Risk Anti-Patterns) regression in the native validation error functions of the Leptos and Dioxus form adapters.
summary.outcome: Successfully refactored `native_validation_error` in both `crates/ars-leptos/src/utility/form.rs` and `crates/ars-dioxus/src/utility/form.rs` by splitting the complex function into smaller branch helpers, resolved a naming collision by renaming `parsed_attr` to `build_parsed_attr`, and committed the fix as `refactor(form): split native validation mapping`. CI was retriggered and the fix was pushed to the remote branch.

repo: The Coverage CI lane takes approximately 26 minutes to complete, which is notably slow compared to other lanes.
The Coverage job failure is gated on CRAP (Change Risk Anti-Patterns) metrics, not just Codecov patch percentage — a separate regression threshold check runs as part of the coverage job.

There is an adapter-boundary lint (`cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`) that catches duplicate helper function names across Leptos and Dioxus adapters — helpers named `parsed_attr` in both adapters triggered it.
The repo uses `build_*` prefix as a naming convention for shared adapter helpers to avoid the cross-adapter duplication lint.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment, and its review state is tracked via GitHub reactions (👀) and review threads.
The project uses `cargo xfmt`, `cargo xclippy`, `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`, and `cargo xcrap` as local validation commands.
wasm targets require `--target wasm32-unknown-unknown` with nightly toolchain; Leptos uses `--features csr` and Dioxus uses `--features web` for wasm builds.

CI checks include formatting, snapshots, clippy, adapter parity, DOM/I18n browser tests, integration, core gates, unit, feature flags (Dioxus/Leptos/subsystems), release verification, and coverage lanes.

code.finding: The `native_validation_error` function was complex enough to trigger CRAP regression; it was split into smaller branch helper functions to reduce per-function complexity without behavioral change.
Same `native_validation_error` CRAP issue as Leptos adapter; same split-into-helpers fix was applied in parallel.

workflow: When the Codecov PR comment hasn't updated yet, the raw GitHub Actions job log (`gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/{id}/logs`) is more reliable for diagnosing coverage failures than waiting for the comment to refresh.
Direct job API checks (`gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/{id}`) can confirm whether a job is genuinely still running versus a stale rollup.
Polling CI state every 120 seconds with combined `gh pr checks`, reactions, and GraphQL review-thread queries is an effective babysitting pattern for slow CI pipelines.
When CRAP gates fail, reducing cyclomatic complexity by splitting functions is preferable to adjusting baselines, as it improves actual code quality.
After formatting touches files, adapter parity lint should be re-run since formatting can affect file state used by the lint.

friction: The Coverage CI lane is slow (~26 minutes), requiring many polling iterations before getting a result.
The Codecov PR comment lags behind the actual job completion, making it unreliable as a status indicator — required falling back to raw job logs.
Running combined test filters in a single `cargo test -p xtask` command with two filters failed due to invalid Cargo syntax, requiring them to be run as separate commands.
The helper split introduced an unintended naming collision (`parsed_attr` in both adapters) that triggered the adapter-boundary lint, requiring an additional rename step before committing.
The CRAP check cannot be run locally reliably because the `lcov.info` file was stale, so remote CI was the only authoritative CRAP result.
open_items: Verify that the CRAP fix (function split) actually passes the remote CRAP gate once the new Coverage CI run completes — this was deferred to CI since local lcov was stale.
Codex review was still in 👀 state at the end of the session — its final review verdict on the refactored form adapters is pending.
The full CI run for the new push had not completed at the end of the session — all lanes need to go green before PR #727 can be merged.
